ab: saalavavala ku, ku daane nai yakai Labuwa, sa mugakungana ku, la talela nai papiya Akau, ikai paihange cekecekecekele.
ch: 小時候我們家住在大武霧台鄉，上學以後，就搬到了屏東，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Labuwa. After I started school, we moved to Pingtung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: sa kadrawngana ku, lai kibulruwa ku ku pathahukai ki kakuakudha ki kacalrisiya, thingalenga ku taikalra makaelaela ku kakuakudha ikaikai Taiwang.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: lakuni, ikai taibalribalriva ki kaladrangedranga, kunaku si kake la mucekele nai kitububulru ki tahudrawdrange, api thingale ku kakuakudhane nai ka Ngudradrekai.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: kaivai tahudrawdrange tahamavalrulunga ku caili ini, ai luceela myaki yacengecenge.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: kyatubulruwa ku iniya ku thingale ini ku vaga, lu ngudradreadrekadha macalrusi, miyalri thingale ku Dridripungana, paihaihangana si amilryalrikana.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的魯凱語說得很流利，也會說日本話、閩南語和英語。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Rukai, as well as Japanese, Hokkien and English.

ab: sa kitubulrungana ku iniya wathingala ku, sika kyabulru saalavavalake ku Dridripungana, ku tauwane ini Amilrika, laikai ki tatwatwatumanane ini kisudalru ku takalra ku paihange, lakuni thingale ku taikalra ka vaga.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是美國人，而他工作上認識許多漢人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an American, and he knows many Chinese at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: sa kitububulru nai iniya, matikai ku taikalra ku lrigila lu alupu kuigayga.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: eene kadruwaka makathingale demedeme, ai ahakai nai ku suci tukacudu, lai makadulrunga nai kilalra tupudriyadringai.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: saalavavalaka ku kadruwa ka wathingale ikai kakuakudhane nai, matininu ku takakacalrisiyanane li.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: sa haukakalra ku thingale li, haungyathathingala ku lu amani madu ku Ngudradrekai, amani ku mwakalrivili, amani ku adulruwane madu ngidradraha.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為魯凱族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Rukai is unique and something to be proud of.
